title: Verified Deep Learning with Lean 4
description: A formal verification of the math of modern deep learning, written as a Lean 4 blueprint project. Mathlib's fderiv as the foundation; VJPs derived rather than asserted; theorems for dense, ReLU, softmax cross-entropy, 2D convolution backward, max-pool, batch norm's three-term backward, residual blocks, depthwise/inverted residuals, squeeze-excitation, layer norm, and a 30-theorem treatment of multi-head self-attention. Eleven chapters span MLP through Vision Transformer, plus a Bestiary chapter mapping the same primitives across vision classifiers, semantic segmentation, image generation, reinforcement learning, NLP, diffusion, and beyond-vision domains (audio, 3D scene reconstruction, scientific). Each chapter pairs proofs with a worked Lean 4 NetSpec that emits StableHLO MLIR -- compiled by IREE for end-to-end training on GPU and CPU -- and is cross-checked against a parallel JAX pipeline. The proof suite contains zero project axioms; every headline theorem closes under exactly {propext, Quot.sound, Classical.choice}, with 51 of those independently kernel-rechecked by leanprover/comparator. Builds on the architectural coverage of 'Convolutional Neural Networks with Swift for TensorFlow' (Apress, 2021), extending through ConvNeXt and ViT and adding formal proofs throughout.
